title: Business Case Template Structure
content: Business case sections: 1 Executive Summary with key numbers; 2 Problem Statement with current situation and business impact; 3 Proposed Solution with alternatives considered; 4 Cost Analysis initial and ongoing; 5 Benefit Analysis quantifiable and non-quantifiable; 6 Financial Analysis; 7 Risks; 8 Recommendation with approval signatures.

title: Simple ROI Calculation Formula
content: ROI percent equals Total Benefits minus Total Costs divided by Total Costs multiplied by 100. Example: Benefits 500000 minus Costs 200000 divided by Costs 200000 multiplied by 100 equals 150 percent ROI. Decision rule: higher ROI means better investment.

title: Payback Period Calculation Formula
content: Payback Period equals Initial Investment divided by Annual Net Cash Flow. Example: Investment 200000 divided by Annual Benefit 80000 equals 2.5 years payback. Shorter payback period means lower risk. Decision rule: payback shorter than project lifecycle is acceptable.

title: Net Present Value NPV Calculation
content: NPV equals sum of Cash Flow t divided by 1 plus discount rate to power of t minus Initial Investment. Where r equals discount rate; t equals time period; n equals number of periods. Decision rule: NPV greater than 0 means accept; NPV less than 0 means reject.

title: Internal Rate of Return IRR Formula
content: IRR is the discount rate that makes NPV equal to zero. Decision rule: IRR greater than Hurdle Rate means accept investment. Typical hurdle rate is 10 to 15 percent. Used to compare multiple investment options against company minimum acceptable return threshold.

title: Benefit-Cost Ratio BCR Formula
content: BCR equals Present Value of Benefits divided by Present Value of Costs. Decision rule: BCR greater than 1 means accept investment. BCR of 2.5 means every dollar spent returns 2.50 dollars in benefit. Higher BCR indicates more efficient investment.

title: Value Lifecycle Framework
content: Four phases: Define Value with business case ROI analysis cost-benefit; Plan Value with success metrics KPIs baseline and targets; Realize Value by tracking benefits reporting progress adjusting; Review Value with post-implementation review lessons learned continuous improvement. Feedback loop connects all phases.

title: Labor Cost Savings Quantification
content: Formula: Hours Saved multiplied by Hourly Rate multiplied by Number of People multiplied by Frequency. Example: 1.5 hours saved per task multiplied by 50 dollars per hour multiplied by 10 people multiplied by 250 working days equals 187500 dollars annual savings. Document all assumptions clearly.

title: Error Reduction Benefit Quantification
content: Formula: Current Error Rate minus New Error Rate multiplied by Volume multiplied by Cost per Error. Example: 5 percent minus 1 percent multiplied by 10000 transactions multiplied by 100 dollars per error equals 40000 dollars annual savings. Requires baseline measurement of current error rate.

title: Revenue Increase Benefit Quantification
content: Formula Option 1: Additional Units multiplied by Price multiplied by Margin. Formula Option 2: Current Revenue multiplied by Expected Percentage Increase. Document assumptions about market conditions and conversion rates. Revenue increases have medium to high uncertainty so use conservative estimates.

title: Cost Avoidance Benefit Quantification
content: Cost avoidance examples: avoiding need to hire additional staff; avoiding regulatory fines; avoiding system replacement costs. Note: cost avoidance is often viewed skeptically by finance stakeholders. Always document assumptions clearly and distinguish from hard cost savings.

title: NPV Calculation Example Table
content: NPV example with 10 percent discount rate: Year 0 investment negative 200000; Year 1 cash flow 80000 discount factor 0.909 present value 72720; Year 2 cash flow 80000 discount factor 0.826 present value 66080; Year 3 cash flow 80000 discount factor 0.751 present value 60080. NPV negative 1120 means reject.

title: Business Case Cost Analysis - Initial Investment
content: One-time cost categories: Software and Licenses; Hardware and Infrastructure; Implementation Services; Internal Resources FTE cost; Training; Data Migration; Change Management; Contingency 15 to 20 percent. Sum all categories for Total Initial Investment amount.

title: Business Case Cost Analysis - Ongoing Annual Costs
content: Annual operating cost categories: Software Maintenance or SaaS subscriptions; Infrastructure and Hosting; Support Resources; Ongoing Training. Sum for Total Annual Operating Cost. Calculate Total Cost of Ownership over 3 years by adding initial investment to annual costs per year.

title: Business Case Benefit Analysis - Quantifiable Benefits
content: Hard benefit categories with annual value: Labor Cost Reduction; Revenue Increase; Error Reduction; Process Efficiency gains; Cost Avoidance. Each benefit needs calculation formula and confidence level High Medium or Low. Sum for Total Quantifiable Benefits.

title: Business Case Benefit Analysis - Non-Quantifiable Benefits
content: Soft benefit examples: Improved customer satisfaction; Better decision-making capability; Competitive advantage; Regulatory compliance; Employee satisfaction and morale. Document these separately from quantifiable benefits. Soft benefits support approval but do not replace financial analysis.

title: KPI Definition Template Structure
content: KPI template sections: Identity with KPI ID name owner and category; Definition with purpose formula and unit of measure; Measurement with data source frequency and reporting lag; Targets with Baseline Target Stretch Goal and Threshold; Visualization with display type and color coding Green Yellow Red.

title: KPI Dashboard Categories
content: KPI dashboard areas: Financial KPIs cost savings revenue ROI; Operational KPIs process time error rate throughput system uptime; Customer KPIs NPS support tickets self-service rate; Adoption KPIs user adoption rate feature usage training completion. Track trend percentage of target achieved.
tags: kpi dashboard financial operational customer adoption

title: Post-Implementation Review PIR Template
content: PIR sections: Review Details date period reviewers; Executive Summary overall assessment and key findings; Objectives Achievement comparison table; Benefits Realization expected vs realized with variance; Budget and Schedule Performance; Stakeholder Satisfaction ratings; Lessons Learned; Outstanding Items with owners and due dates.

title: Benefits Realization Tracking
content: Track benefits over time comparing expected to actual. Realization table columns: Expected Benefit; Expected Value; Realized Value; Variance amount and percentage. Explain variances. Recommend actions to close gaps. Benefits realization is ongoing not a one-time post-go-live activity.

title: Solution Evaluation Checklist - Pre-Implementation
content: Pre-implementation checklist: business case documented and approved; ROI and financial metrics calculated; success criteria defined as SMART; KPIs identified with baselines; target values agreed with stakeholders; measurement approach defined; data sources identified. Complete before project starts.

title: Solution Evaluation Checklist - Post-Implementation
content: Post-implementation checklist: PIR scheduled typically 3 to 6 months post go-live; actual benefits measured; comparison to business case completed; lessons learned documented; continuous improvement actions identified; knowledge transferred to operations team.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for Business Case
content: Sensitivity analysis tests how ROI changes when key assumptions change. Vary one assumption at a time: if adoption rate drops from 90 to 70 percent ROI changes from 150 to 90 percent. Identify which assumptions have most impact on ROI. Present best case base case and worst case scenarios.

title: Break-Even Analysis
content: Break-even point is when total benefits equal total costs. Calculate: Break-even point equals Initial Investment divided by Net Annual Benefit per period. Plot cumulative costs and benefits over time. Break-even visualizes when investment starts paying back and validates payback period calculation.

title: Risk-Adjusted Returns
content: Adjust expected benefits by confidence level and probability of realization. Formula: Risk-Adjusted Benefit equals Expected Benefit multiplied by Probability of achieving benefit. Example: 100000 expected benefit with 70 percent confidence equals 70000 risk-adjusted benefit. Use for conservative business case.

title: Value Case vs Business Case Distinction
content: Business case focuses on financial metrics ROI NPV payback period. Value case includes both financial and strategic value: market positioning; capability building; risk reduction; regulatory compliance. Value case is broader and more persuasive for strategic investments with uncertain financial returns.

title: Alternatives Considered Table in Business Case
content: Alternatives table columns: Option; Pros; Cons; Why Not Selected. Options typically include: Do Nothing - no cost but problems persist unacceptable; Option A with pros and cons; Option B Recommended; Option C with reason not selected. Show you evaluated multiple options rigorously.

title: Solution Evaluation Checklist - Ongoing
content: Ongoing checklist: benefits tracked over time; KPI dashboard maintained with current data; regular reviews with stakeholders conducted; value optimization opportunities identified and acted on. Benefits realization is a continuous process throughout solution lifetime not just at go-live.

title: SMART Success Criteria for Solutions
content: Success criteria must be SMART: Specific clearly defined outcome; Measurable with quantifiable metric; Achievable realistic given constraints; Relevant aligned to business objective; Time-bound with target date. Example: Reduce processing time from 4 hours to 1 hour by end of Q2 measured weekly via system report.

title: Business Case Executive Summary Section
content: Executive Summary includes: 2-3 paragraphs covering Problem Solution Investment and Expected Return. Key Numbers box: Total Investment amount; Expected Annual Benefit amount; Payback Period in months; 3-Year ROI percentage. This section is read first so make it compelling and quantified.

title: Benefit Quantification - Process Efficiency Gains
content: Quantify process efficiency by measuring time reduction. Steps: baseline current process time; project future state time; calculate time saved per transaction; multiply by volume and hourly rate. Include both direct labor savings and indirect benefits like faster customer response time.

title: Stakeholder Satisfaction in PIR
content: PIR stakeholder satisfaction table: Stakeholder Group; Rating 1-5; Key Feedback summary. Groups: End Users; Management; IT and Support; Customers if applicable. Use to identify adoption issues early. Low satisfaction scores indicate need for additional change management or training investment.

title: Lessons Learned in Post-Implementation Review
content: Lessons learned structure: What Worked Well - list 3 successes; What Could Be Improved - list 3 improvement areas; Recommendations for Future Projects - actionable guidance. Distribute lessons learned to project management office for institutional learning and future project planning.

title: Cost Estimation Approaches
content: Three estimation approaches: Top-Down uses analogous data from similar projects; Bottom-Up breaks work into tasks and sums estimates; Parametric uses statistical models such as cost per function point. Use multiple approaches and triangulate. Add 15-20 percent contingency for uncertainty.

title: Problem Statement in Business Case
content: Problem statement sections: Current Situation describing the problem or opportunity; Business Impact of Status Quo with 3 quantified impacts; Root Cause explaining why problem exists. Quantify impact where possible: costs 500000 per year in rework; 2 hours per transaction wasted; 15 percent customer churn from poor experience.

title: KPI Color Coding for Dashboard
content: Dashboard color coding convention: Green means above target performing well; Yellow means between threshold and target needs attention; Red means below threshold requires immediate action. Define specific numeric thresholds for each color band. Color coding enables quick executive review of solution health.

title: Business Case Risk Register
content: Risk register in business case: columns Risk ID; Description; Probability High Medium Low; Impact High Medium Low; Mitigation Strategy. Common risks: low user adoption; technical integration issues; scope expansion; key personnel leaving; vendor delays. Risk-adjusted ROI uses probability in calculations.

title: Intangible Benefit Documentation Strategy
content: Document intangible benefits with supporting evidence: customer satisfaction surveys for satisfaction claims; employee retention data for morale claims; market research for competitive advantage claims. Convert to monetary where possible using willingness-to-pay or replacement cost methods.
